review_title: The one program that I can't live without
description: Overall it is one of the few programs that I tend to "nerd out" over. I am always thinking "this would be better in Airtable" when I am in a spreadsheet or another database. I have created Bases that run my business, create invoices, track my continuing education credits, budget, CRM's, organize my home, etc. If it can go in a spreadsheet, it can become so much more in an Airtable Base. It is the one piece of software that I don't even think twice about renewing. And now that they have integrated AI into its capabilities, I'm even more excited to try using it for other projects.
author: Dana K.
pros: I love the flexibility of Airtable. You can design the relational database of your dreams with no programming skills necessary. It's easy to use and has terrific automations that can increase the power of your data.
cons: There isn't much that I don't like. The one small thing that I can think of is that when you are adding records in a table view, it would be nice if hitting Enter would automatically start a new record instead of giving the popup saying to press Shift-Enter. It especially should not erase the record if you hit Enter a second time.

review_title: A Very Powerful Tool with Many Applications
description: Overall I'm very satisfied with Airtable. My team and I all use it on a daily basis. It allows us to integrate all of our types of data and functions into one place, making it much more powerful. We also integrate Airtable with Zapier, Documint, and other platforms when needed.
author: Andy B.
pros: We use Airtable for a huge variety of functions, like data management, project management, content management, CRM, and more. Airtable is extremely customizable.
cons: I don't have many cons. Occasionally we bump into an advanced function that we would like to achieve that Airtable doesn't support. That said, there are usually ways to work around it and they are continually adding new features.

review_title: Terrible account management and hidden charges - do not use
description: Absolutely horrible - there is no amount of good tech that is worth dealing with this horrible account management. They do not care about customer service or their clients whatsoever.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The overall functionality of the tool achieves what we need it to do and the senior leadership likes the dashboard functionality
cons: Horrific customer service. The worst I have ever seen. Tech team is only available via email and there is a clear language barrier causing issues in communicating programs and finding solutions. Account management is abhorrent. They are not proactive and actual blame the client for their lack of skilled account managers. When issues are escalated to senior leadership you are met with blame and rudeness. Financial processes are a mess. Late invoicing, demanding immediate payment. There is no functionality in place that prevents you from going over seats/licenses and this is by design - it allows Airtable to continually hit you with overage fees.

review_title: Beautiful, Flexible UI Held Back by Limited Schema Export
description: Overall, my experience with Airtable has been very positive, and it has become one of my favorite tools to work in every day. It hits a great balance between spreadsheet simplicity and database power, with a UI that makes complex data feel approachable and lets me build useful tools for my team without heavy development work.
author: Cristiano F.
pros: What I like most about Airtable is the user experience: the interface feels modern, clean, and very visual, so it’s easy to understand even for non‑technical users. I love how you can switch between different views (grid, calendar, gallery, kanban) and build simple interfaces on top of your data, which makes everyday work feel smooth and actually enjoyable.
cons: The main disappointment for me is that Airtable focuses on exporting and importing data, but not really on exporting or importing the structure of a base. There’s no simple, native way to move a full schema (tables, fields, relationships) between workspaces or to back it up, so you often end up recreating the structure by hand or relying on scripts and workarounds.

review_title: Thanks to you Airtable!
description: This is amazing tool especially in task management because it provides an easy to use which is why I loved most of using this tool it really helped me in my everyday task.
author: Rex S.
pros: I love using Airtable because of the task management that the tools has. All files are being stored in a safest way possible and also the email notifications does his work which is why I used it frequently.
cons: I think what I didn't like about Airtable is that the tools will sometimes doesn't work due to technical issues and I think it is understandble on their end.

review_title: We use Airtable frequently
description: This has prevented our team from losing links in everyone’s messy inbox. Although, there may be better ways to design the interface and feel at times like it is a little cumbersome, this program does bring some semblance of order to our chaotic processes.
author: Keith S.
pros: I enjoyed the switch to be able to flip back and forth between grid view and gallery view on the swap button, so my older guys could see how they would go with it in a practice setting. I was impressed with how well the AI tool build off of my initial prompt and created a proper starting point for me, it also looked fairly good.
cons: The mobile app next to it is slow when many records are there in the loading. Automation Scripting, too many technical terms things normal people could never learn for themselves.

review_title: Fun to use, pretty to look at, functional and feature-rich tool
description: Airtable makes managing data pretty and functional in ways simple spreadsheets simply cannot. With their multiple views, integrated data, and smart capabilities (not to mention the AI features!), it's a solid tool worth playing with.
author: Dan J.
pros: I love the way the views of data are customizable to serve the specific needs you have at the time. Different teams required different components of the same dataset to be visible while accessing, and Airtable made it easy to create filtered views for each.
cons: For those less tech-savvy, Airtable felt a little complex (rightfully so). It can do a lot and requires proper thought and planning for long-term use and configuration.

review_title: Highly customizable management software with a learning curve
author: Katherine G.
pros: Airtable is incredibly customizable. It can be used for a variety of tasks, from project management, contact libraries, inventory tracking, grant-tracking, to-do lists, comms planning, etc. It is really a jack-of-all-trades product.
cons: There is a steep user-curve with Airtable. The more tech-savvy members of my team love it, but others have disliked it, even just navigating an already-created Airtable. You have to set aside dedicated time to bring people on board to use Airtable, even as viewers. Some people may find it too complicated to learn to create their own tables.

review_title: Easy to Integrate- Powerful on the Backend
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Airtable is highly integratable and allows our team to enhance our product for our users. They don't see Airtable but it's doing a ton of things in the background- making it easier for our developers and users.
cons: I don't have any negative feedback to share at this moment. Everything has been really great and the integration was easy.

review_title: Good automation program
description: To begin with no experience is a challenge. Customer service is great. Very helpful kind and courteous
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Airtable has great potential for workflow automation. For monthly plan its worth it if use all the time.
cons: I had issues figuring everything out. Then read up on how to use it. Which helped. I like the options it has tho
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Airtable - the simplicity of a spreadsheet with the structured capabilities of a database
description: overall airtable helped our business grow exponentially not just because of its applications but it gave us the perspective about what database can do for a business and how simple it can be if we keep that way. we used it for our app database as well, and it just worked fine bit slow but good for beta version.
author: Dhruv J.
pros: What I love most: The intuitive interface makes it easy to customize views (grid, calendar, kanban, form) and build relationships between different tables. The automation features are a game-changer, saving me countless hours on repetitive tasks. Who would benefit: Anyone looking for a more dynamic and collaborative way to manage data. It's perfect for teams of all sizes and across various industries.
cons: One of the least best things about Airtable is its pricing. While there is a free plan, it has limitations on the number of records and storage space. The paid plans can become expensive, especially for larger teams or those with extensive data needs. AI plans are also overpriced.

review_title: Game Changer
description: Overall, it helped us automate a lot of our processes and work with large spreadsheets without the complications of Excel. Definitely recommended if you're looking for a low-code app to solve basic business needs.
author: Aryan S.
pros: Intuitive and user-friendly interface. It has powerful database capabilities with spreadsheet simplicity that is highly customizable with automations and integrations. The real-time collaboration and seamless team workflow allows it to scale with business needs. It was truly a game changer for my Small business.
cons: Advanced features require higher tier plans that I was not aware of originally. Also, some integrations need third-party tolls like Zapier to fully unlock the power of Airtable.

review_title: My Experience with Airtable
author: Kelvin K.
pros: Airtable allocates a comprehensive set of capabilities ranging from resource management, project collaboration, file sharing to project management in one central repository.
cons: Ever since I implemented Airtable, I have never come across any drawbacks.

review_title: Airtable - More User Friendly Than Excel
description: Overall, it's been a great experience. My favorite aspect is you can embed a file such as an image file in one of the "cells"
author: Nis J.
pros: It's like an enhanced version of excel, but much more intuitive and not relying on formulas.
cons: The nomenclature can sometimes be confusing.

review_title: Comprehensive PM/TM and Project Collaboration Software
description: Collaborating on projects help save time by allowing remote teams in my organization to work together from anywhere.
author: Stephen W.
pros: Airtable automates over 85% of manual business processes ranging from project management, task management and content marketing - this is the best thing that I like most so far.
cons: Ever since I started using Airtable to plan, track and manage projects, I have never come across an issue that lasted for over 24 hours unresolved.

review_title: Airtable is the Real PM & TM G.O.A.T
description: Airtable is a robust tool that I have had a remarkable experience with for nearly six years now. It is my go-to solution for TM, PM and marketing planning.
author: Martha B.
pros: As BPM, I must oversee the future of my organization by acquiring as many partners as the work can give. I like that Airtable offers marketing planning tools that aid in my day-to-day activities. In addition, Airtable manage task and projects through automation of manual workflow.
cons: I am fully contented with the features that I access via Airtable since they accomplish my needs without any pressure.

review_title: Airtable Review by Optometrist
description: Real-time collaboration on projects is one of the best aspects of using Airtable. Automating repetitive tasks is another benefits that I realize.
author: Charles O.
pros: Airtable facilitates all the features that allows me to track, plan and organize optometric projects automatically across my two current organizations.
cons: Ever since 2021 when I started using Airtable, I have never come across daunting aspects and I use it on a daily basis.

review_title: Versatile database for property and client management.
description: Listings, client data, and follow-ups were kept at the forefront and up-to-date, project collaboration was improved with clear deadlines, and agents were able to rapidly re-prioritize and transfer, losing fewer opportunities and gaining greater client satisfaction.
author: Ignacio S.
pros: Spreadsheet-like workspace keeps listings, contacts, deals, and follow-ups organized with detailed calendar and kanban views; real-time collaboration allows agents and assistants to see tasks, deals, and updates across unrelated properties and partner teams.
cons: Advanced relational and reporting capabilities need precise configuration, and multi-user, high-volume implementations can bog down at scale or require additional permission management for secure deal tracking.

review_title: Airtable for Social
author: Mercedes S.
pros: Ease of use, project management, project collaboration, tracking, and artifact handling. Building robust editorial calendars, content tracking, and publishing.
cons: Onboarding teams and getting them used to using a new tool. Folks breaking filters, coding, and views.

review_title: Amazed with Airtable
author: Madeline O.
pros: Airtable is so user-friendly and customizable. I am amazed what has been possible using the free version. I am so impressed. I used it as a CRM and it changed my business.
cons: Onboarding others was a bit challenging because it was a new yet powerful product. It was not as collaborative as I needed.

review_title: Flexible Project Tool for QA & Dev Teams
description: It is great for team collaboration and project tracking, intuitive interface, though pricing and advanced reporting are considerations.
author: Clara R.
pros: It is a flexible and visual tool for task and project management, in my QA team it boosts collaboration and helps teams stay organized efficiently.
cons: Advanced features require paid plans, and reporting/diagram tools can feel limited for complex projects.

review_title: Powerful but getting tricky
description: I think it’s fantastic, I’ve been using it for a while now and I keep learning new ways to automate stuff day by day.
author: Bart H.
pros: I like that you can build your database from scratch, kinda like Excel but with more options to setup automations.
cons: I do find it’s getting more and more complicated, like linking databases is harder now, and some things are just not that simple to use anymore.

review_title: Fantastic, vital piece of software
description: Airtable is a vital part of our business activity. We use airtable every minute of every day to record and view client data. We have integrated Airtable to various other software such as Xero in order to help us with invoicing too.
author: Sannah M.
pros: I like being able to track all our projects and plan our business activities on this software.
cons: The views and forms can get complicated sometimes and require a lot of sifting/filtering in order to get the information you want.

review_title: I like Airtable
description: I use Airtable because I work in marketing and is a super useful tool is you need to manage accounts.
author: Marian V.
pros: This platform is super easy to use, you don't need much time to learn how to use it.
cons: It is not the cheapest option, and also is hard to manage a lot of projects, but the pros are more than the cons.

review_title: Flexible and User-Friendly Project Management with Airtable
description: My overall experience with Airtable has been great. It’s a highly flexible and intuitive tool that makes organizing data and managing projects simple and efficient. It’s especially useful for collaboration and creating customized workflows to fit specific needs
author: Abdulrahman M.
pros: What I like most about Airtable is its flexibility and ease of use. It combines the power of a database with the simplicity of a spreadsheet, allowing for customized workflows, data organization, and collaboration. The interface is intuitive, making it easy to manage projects and track tasks efficiently.
cons: The only downside to Airtable is that some advanced features require a paid plan, and it can get a bit complex when managing large datasets
